description: Orchestrator + Skills refactor, phases 5-9: Guardrail Agent (input-side content gate), Data Manager Agent (data-side governance) + shared data_catalog skill, firewall middleware on every inter-agent transit, floating helper skills (web_browser, acropedia) wired via LangChain tool-calling, and cleanup sweep. All work lands on top of phases 0a-4 already completed on the same branch; spec is docs/specs/2026-04-23-orchestrator-skills-refactor-design.md.

userStories.title: Add GuardrailVerdict type to models/types.py
userStories.description: As the Guardrail Agent, I need a typed return value so callers can branch on pass/reject deterministically.
acceptanceCriteria: models/types.py defines `class GuardrailVerdict(BaseModel)` with fields: `passed: bool`, `reason: str = ''`, `redacted_question: str`
Type imports cleanly (no circular imports) from agents/ modules
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Landed in phase-0a-langchain-async as commits 4136299, 7c0c47d, 50ace54, ea48cde, 585d241.

userStories.title: Create skills/workflow/relevance_check.md
userStories.description: As the Guardrail Agent, I need markdown instructions for judging whether a reviewer question is in-scope for case review.
acceptanceCriteria: File skills/workflow/relevance_check.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter (name, description, type=workflow, owner=[guardrail_agent], mode=inline)
Body explains: in-scope = credit-risk / case-review questions; out-of-scope examples include 'what to eat for lunch' and personal-chat topics
Body specifies JSON output shape: {passed: bool, reason: str}
skills.loader.load_skill() parses the file without raising SkillLoadError
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Landed in phase-0a-langchain-async as commits 4136299, 7c0c47d, 50ace54, ea48cde, 585d241.

userStories.title: Create skills/workflow/redact.md
userStories.description: As the Guardrail Agent and future Data Manager Agent, I need shared markdown instructions for redacting identifiers from inbound text.
acceptanceCriteria: File skills/workflow/redact.md exists with YAML frontmatter (type=workflow, owner=[guardrail_agent, data_manager], mode=inline) — owner list must include both agents
Body describes identifier patterns to mask (raw account numbers, 6+-digit runs, CASE-XXXXX IDs, role-injection tokens [SYSTEM]/[USER])
Body specifies JSON output shape: {redacted: str, masked_spans: list[str]}
skills.loader.load_skill() parses the file
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Landed in phase-0a-langchain-async as commits 4136299, 7c0c47d, 50ace54, ea48cde, 585d241.

userStories.title: Build agents/guardrail_agent.py with screen() method
userStories.description: As main.py, I need a GuardrailAgent whose screen(question) method redacts identifiers and decides relevance before the Orchestrator runs.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/guardrail_agent.py defines `class GuardrailAgent` with `__init__(self, llm: FirewalledModel, logger: EventLogger)` and `async def screen(self, question: str) -> GuardrailVerdict`
screen() invokes redact.md first (inline prompt) to produce a redacted question, then invokes relevance_check.md to decide pass/reject
Off-topic questions (e.g., 'what should I eat for lunch?') return GuardrailVerdict(passed=False, reason=...)
tests/test_agents/test_guardrail_agent.py covers: in-scope pass, off-topic reject, redaction of 6+-digit run, redaction of CASE-XXXXX token
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Landed in phase-0a-langchain-async as commits 4136299, 7c0c47d, 50ace54, ea48cde, 585d241.

userStories.title: Wire Guardrail into main.py ahead of Orchestrator.run()
userStories.description: As a reviewer, my questions should be screened by the Guardrail Agent before any orchestration work begins — rejected questions return a friendly message and skip Orchestrator.run().
acceptanceCriteria: main.py constructs a GuardrailAgent alongside ChatAgent
Every path that previously called run_question() now calls GuardrailAgent.screen() first
On GuardrailVerdict.passed == False, main prints reason and skips Orchestrator.run()
On pass, Orchestrator.run() receives the redacted question (not the raw)
Interactive REPL loop handles reject without exiting — reviewer can try another question
pytest tests/ passes
userStories.notes: Landed in phase-0a-langchain-async as commits 4136299, 7c0c47d, 50ace54, ea48cde, 585d241.

userStories.title: Create skills/workflow/data_catalog.md
userStories.description: As the Orchestrator and the Data Manager Agent, I need markdown instructions for querying the catalog tool and interpreting its results.
acceptanceCriteria: File skills/workflow/data_catalog.md exists with YAML frontmatter (type=workflow, owner=[orchestrator, data_manager], mode=inline) — owner list must include both
Body describes the catalog tool surface: list_available_tables, get_table_schema, column descriptions
Body explains how orchestrator uses catalog for team_construction (column coverage) and synthesis (grounding evidence in real table/column names)
skills.loader.load_skill() parses the file
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Build agents/data_manager_agent.py with query() and describe_catalog()
userStories.description: As Base Specialists and the Orchestrator, I need a Data Manager that fronts the gateway — applies redact policy on returned data and owns the data_catalog skill.
acceptanceCriteria: agents/data_manager_agent.py defines `class DataManagerAgent` with `__init__(self, gateway, catalog, llm, logger)` and `async def query(self, table: str, filters: dict) -> QueryResult`, `async def describe_catalog(self) -> str`
query() wraps gateway.query_table and applies redact.md policy on output text fields before returning
describe_catalog() returns the catalog prompt-context using the data_catalog.md skill as system prompt guidance
Tests in tests/test_agents/test_data_manager_agent.py cover: query() happy path, query() redacts digit runs, describe_catalog() returns catalog text
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Orchestrator loads data_catalog.md for team_construction + synthesis
userStories.description: As the Orchestrator, my team selection and synthesis prompts should include the data_catalog skill body — currently the catalog is injected ad-hoc via `catalog.to_prompt_context()`; move the instruction text into the markdown skill.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator module loads data_catalog.md body at import (mirroring how team_construction.md is loaded)
The body is appended to the system prompt in _select_team and synthesize
Existing orchestrator tests still pass (behavior is additive — more instruction text, same output shape)
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Add FirewallStack.send() for inter-agent transit
userStories.description: As the firewall, I need to intercept inter-agent messages (not just LLM calls) so redaction and logging apply on every cross-agent edge.
acceptanceCriteria: gateway/firewall_stack.py adds `async def send(self, message, from_agent: str, to_agent: str) -> <same type as message>` that logs the transit and returns the (possibly-redacted) message
send() applies the redact.md policy when the message contains string fields with identifier patterns
send() validates message shape via Pydantic when the message is a BaseModel instance
tests/test_gateway/test_firewall_stack.py adds coverage for send() on: plain dict, Pydantic model, message containing a 6+-digit run
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Wrap Orchestrator.run's cross-agent calls with firewall.send
userStories.description: As the firewall bus, every agent-to-agent handoff inside Orchestrator.run should route through send() — not just LLM calls.
acceptanceCriteria: Inside Orchestrator.run, the ReportDraft returned from report_agent.run() is routed through firewall.send(...)
TeamDraft returned from _run_team_workflow is routed through firewall.send(...)
FinalAnswer handed back from balance() is routed through firewall.send(...)
Event log (logger) shows one transit event per cross-agent edge
Existing tests pass with no behavior change on clean inputs
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Add configurable asyncio.Semaphore to FirewalledModel for LLM rate limiting
userStories.description: As the orchestrator fanning out parallel branches + parallel specialists, I need a shared semaphore so we don't hammer the LLM API on fan-out.
acceptanceCriteria: FirewallStack.__init__ accepts `concurrency_cap: int = 8` and creates an `asyncio.Semaphore(concurrency_cap)`
FirewalledModel.ainvoke acquires the semaphore before each LLM call and releases in a try/finally
build_llm() exposes concurrency_cap via kwarg (like api_max_retries)
Test: constructing FirewallStack with concurrency_cap=2 and launching 4 parallel ainvoke calls completes correctly (no deadlock, all 4 return)
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Create skills/helper/web_browser.md and skills/helper/acropedia.md
userStories.description: As Chat Agent, Guardrail Agent, and Base Specialists, I need tool-callable helper skill files to reference.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/helper/web_browser.md exists with frontmatter type=helper, mode=tool, tool_signature='web_browser(url: str) -> str', owner=[chat_agent, guardrail_agent, base_specialist]
skills/helper/acropedia.md exists with frontmatter type=helper, mode=tool, tool_signature='acropedia_lookup(term: str) -> dict', owner=[chat_agent, guardrail_agent, base_specialist]
acropedia.md body describes the lookup contract (input: abbreviation/term; output: {full_name, explanation}) and notes that the underlying adapter may be a stub initially
web_browser.md body can be a brief placeholder
skills.loader.load_skills_for('chat_agent') returns both helpers
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Build tools/acropedia.py adapter stub
userStories.description: As the acropedia_lookup tool, I need a Python implementation that's pluggable to a future internal platform but returns safe stub data today.
acceptanceCriteria: tools/acropedia.py defines `def acropedia_lookup(term: str) -> dict` returning {'full_name': str, 'explanation': str}
Stub returns a well-known canned response for 'DTI' (debt-to-income) and 'FICO' so tests can assert specific values
Unknown terms return {'full_name': term, 'explanation': 'Definition not available in Acropedia stub'}
tests/test_tools/test_acropedia.py covers: known term (DTI), unknown term fallback
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Wire helpers as LangChain @tool callables on agents' LLM
userStories.description: As Chat Agent, Guardrail Agent, and Base Specialists, I should be able to call acropedia_lookup and web_browser when the LLM decides I need them.
acceptanceCriteria: New helper in gateway/llm_factory.py (or agents/helpers.py) returns LangChain @tool-decorated versions of acropedia_lookup and a placeholder web_browser callable
ChatAgent constructor optionally accepts a list of bound tools and binds them via model.bind_tools(...) on its FirewalledModel
Test: ChatAgent with acropedia_lookup bound can handle a mocked LLM that emits a tool_call for acropedia_lookup, and the result is fed back into the conversation
Tool binding is additive — ChatAgent without tools still works as today
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Remove dead mode == 'report' branches from orchestrator + plan_team
userStories.description: The --mode CLI flag was dropped in Phase 4 but plan_team still has a `mode == 'report'` branch. Remove it.
acceptanceCriteria: orchestrator.plan_team no longer accepts a `mode` parameter OR the `mode == 'report'` branch is removed (keeping the kwarg for compat is fine if it's purely pass-through to specialists)
orchestrator._run_team_workflow continues to hard-code mode='chat' internally
test_plan_team_report_mode_returns_all_with_root_question test is either deleted or rewritten to cover the non-report path
grep shows no remaining `mode == 'report'` or `'report'` references in orchestrator/orchestrator.py
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Delete or consolidate chat_agent.format_for_reviewer
userStories.description: main.py now uses a local _format_final_answer for FinalAnswer rendering. The old ChatAgent.format_for_reviewer (which formatted FinalOutput) may be unused.
acceptanceCriteria: grep shows no production call to ChatAgent.format_for_reviewer outside of its own test file
Either: delete format_for_reviewer + its tests, OR extend it to accept FinalAnswer and migrate main._format_final_answer into ChatAgent (pick one, land it consistently)
All existing tests for ChatAgent still pass (or are updated to reflect the chosen API)
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Drop the legacy FinalOutput in favor of TeamDraft
userStories.description: FinalOutput and TeamDraft are duplicates. Orchestrator.synthesize still emits FinalOutput and _run_team_workflow adapts it to TeamDraft. Collapse into TeamDraft.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator.synthesize returns TeamDraft directly (not FinalOutput)
_run_team_workflow no longer needs the field-by-field adapter
FinalOutput class is deleted from models/types.py, OR aliased `FinalOutput = TeamDraft` with a deprecation comment if external callers exist
All existing tests updated to reference TeamDraft
pytest tests/ passes

userStories.title: Refresh brainstorm/2026-04-23-architecture-current.md snapshot
userStories.description: The current-architecture mermaid snapshot was written before phases 0a-4 landed. Update it to reflect the new parallel Reports+Team flow with Guardrail on the input side and Data Manager on the data side.
acceptanceCriteria: brainstorm/2026-04-23-architecture-current.md mermaid diagram shows: Reviewer → Guardrail → ChatAgent → Orchestrator.run → asyncio.gather(ReportAgent, TeamWorkflow) → Balance → FinalAnswer
Diagram includes Data Manager Agent between specialists and the gateway
Diagram shows FirewallStack wrapping both LLM calls and inter-agent transits
Notes section updates to reflect async + LangChain + skills-as-markdown state (not the sync + Python-modules state the original snapshot described)
pytest tests/ passes (this story only touches docs, but run anyway as sanity check)
